rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although a pleading should stand on its own and not refer to or attach copies of prior pleadings. It also does not reflect well on a fiduciary to file copies of notes from the Court raising issues with the fiduciary's accountings, which issues, if raised twice in the past, he should have anticipated and addressed in his current accounting.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE OWYANG REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 26, 2000 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust; For Accounting; To Appoint Successor Trustee; For Surcharge; For Imposition Of Constructive Trust; And For Emotional Damages
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine how best to proceed. Counsel for the successor trustee did not file an ex parte petition to consolidate this case with the already existing case, PTR-14-298278, until 8/26/21, despite having been instructed by the Court to do so on 7/12/21. The ex parte "application" was not actually E-filed until 9/2/21 and, following this Court's procedures, assigned on the following Tuesday, 9/7/21. Although Examiners have discretion to review regular ex parte petitions as time permits, because this "application" was assigned to this Examiner and because this hearing was coming up, this Examiner chose to review the ex parte "application" immediately. The "application", however, seeks to have this newer case number survive; Probate cases are always consolidated with the earlier/earliest case number surviving, in which an estate or trust was first brought before the Court. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD CHESTER PRATT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/8/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Still no proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] Proposed orders should NOT be E-filed. (Notice does not need to be given of an unsigned/unfiled proposed order that is not being heard.) A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a).] (A petitioner does not need to give himself notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 1201.] Although this continuance has to be to 11/8/21, that is well beyond the 120 days requested in the petition verified on 3/22/2021. If a petition for final distribution is not filed before 11/8/21 (or a proposed order is not submitted well before 11/8/21), an appearance will be required on 11/8/21. Only one courtesy copy of each paper is required to be provided, not two. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM M ROTEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Probate Code section 12251(a) provides in relevant part that: "If it appears there is no property of any kind belonging to the estate and subject to administration, the personal representative may petition for the termination of further proceedings and for discharge of the personal representative." The petition alleges that the total value of the decedent's bank accounts without designated beneficiaries is $42,000, collectable in accordance with the small estate affidavit procedure under sections 13100 et seq. without continuing this probate administration. According to the petition, there are no assets on hand available for distribution, and the creditor whose claim is on file will be paid from the funds collected by the beneficiaries. The amended declaration of the petitioner , however, states that he issued an "estate check" to the creditor in full satisfaction of the claim. Has the petitioner already collected the $42,000 from the decedent's bank accounts? Is the petitioner holding the funds remaining in the estate checking account after payment of the creditor? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2021 to be heard with other petition. 1) On 04/16/2021 a Declaration signed by Petitioner alleges Decedent's name is Aleksander Aleksandrovich Donskoi and in some business dealings his last name was misspelled Donskoy. It appears an amended petition must be filed with Decedent's correct name with an aka and must be re-noticed and re-published. (CRC 7.53). After the amended petition is filed it appears an ex parte must be filed to correct the name of Decedent in the court system. 2) Attorney John Saadeh filed DE-147S the Confidential statement several times as part of other documents that are not confidential. Unclear if attorney will be filing to remove the DE-147S from non-confidential filings. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/13/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

[LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/4/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VIRGINIA C MEHEGAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No proof of service on the special administrator of the settlor's estate is on file. The special administrator has not waived formal service, nor has he consented to the relief sought by the petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GLORIA S. STEVENS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determing Trust'S Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/8/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Under what authority did the executor of the will of Richard Stevens quitclaim Richard Stevens's interest in the real property to the deceased spouse (not even to the trustee of her trust)? If he is the executor of Richard Stevens's will, there should be an order authorizing distribution. Furthermore, Richard Stevens's will provides for a testamentary trust, not outright distribution. The petitioner is also the trustee of his deceased sister's trust. When did Peggy Stevens die? Who are the beneficiaries of her trust? They must be identified and must receive notice. All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (The [successor] trustee's counsel's office does not appear to be in San Francisco, as alleged in paragraph 3 of the petition, although that is irrelevant to venue.) Whether the petition is supplemented or amended to address these issues, notice with a copy of the supplement or amendment must be served. [CRC 7.53(b).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/29/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/13/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/27/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELEN MEYER LANTZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. petitioner may be asked to state whether a probate was opened for the predeceased husband, how title to the RP is held, to address the inconsistent dates in the will, and to address his experience with probate and a proper estate administration. please note, this court generally does not appoint persons acting in pro per with full authority and no bond. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.
